duo.savedMsg: Your Duo Plan accounts have been saved.
duo.modalTitle: Which accounts do you want to add?

diversif.title: Diversification
diversif.body: Don't put all your eggs in one basket. If you have 10 companies across different sectors and one goes bankrupt, you lose 10%. Diversification spreads the risk.
diversif.tip: Do you have more than 30% of your portfolio in a single sector?

dca.title: Dollar Cost Averaging
dca.body: Investing the same amount every month, regardless of whether the market goes up or down. When it drops, you buy more shares for the same price. Discipline beats timing.
dca.tip: Set a fixed monthly amount and automate it.

dividendo.title: Dividends
dividendo.body: Some companies pay you just for holding their shares. Automatically reinvesting dividends can double your return over 20 years.
dividendo.tip: Look for companies with 10+ years of consecutive dividend increases.

pe_ratio.title: P/E Ratio
pe_ratio.body: The P/E compares a stock's price to what the company earns. There's no universal 'good' number — compare it against the sector and its history.
pe_ratio.tip: A very high P/E doesn't mean expensive if the company is growing fast. Context beats the number.

moat.title: Competitive Advantage
moat.body: The 'moat' is what makes a company hard to copy: brand (Apple), network (Visa), low costs (Walmart). It protects its margins over time.
moat.tip: What would stop a competitor with $1B from taking this market?

aversion.title: Loss Aversion
aversion.body: The brain feels the pain of losing $100 twice as strongly as the pleasure of gaining $100. That's why we sell in a panic. Recognizing this bias is the first step.
aversion.tip: Before selling on a dip: did the business fundamentals actually change?

rebalanceo.title: Rebalancing
rebalanceo.body: If your stocks went up a lot, you now have more risk than you wanted. Rebalancing means selling what grew and buying what fell behind.
rebalanceo.tip: Rebalance 1-2 times a year, or whenever something drifts more than 5% from your target.

fund.title: Free Cash Flow
fund.body: Earnings can be manipulated through accounting. Free Cash Flow doesn't lie — it's the real cash coming into the company's coffers.
fund.tip: Look for companies whose FCF grows faster than their revenue.

horizonte.title: Investment Horizon
horizonte.body: The market drops 10-20% about once every 1-2 years. But over 10+ years, the S&P 500 has never lost money. Your horizon determines how much risk you can absorb.
horizonte.tip: Need the money in less than 3 years? It shouldn't be in stocks.

fomo.title: Financial FOMO
fomo.body: Fear Of Missing Out makes you buy at the top. FOMO is the most reliable signal that an asset is overvalued. The best time to buy is when nobody's talking about it.
fomo.tip: If it's all over the news and your family's WhatsApp — be careful.

etf.title: ETFs vs Stocks
etf.body: An ETF replicates an entire index. 90% of active managers do NOT beat the index over 15 years. ETFs are the smart starting point.
etf.tip: Start with broad ETFs. Add individual stocks only once you understand the business.

comprar_negocios.title: Buy Businesses
comprar_negocios.body: Before buying a stock: do I understand how this company makes money? Would I still be buying if the market closed for 5 years?
comprar_negocios.tip: Warren Buffett only invests in what he can understand in 5 minutes.

bull_bear.title: Drops Are Normal
bull_bear.body: Since 1950, the S&P 500 has fallen more than 10% about once a year on average. Despite that, it multiplied 200x. Drops are the price of returns.
bull_bear.tip: Keep a list of companies you'd want to buy when they fall.

concentracion.title: Concentration Risk
concentracion.body: More than 20% in a single company or more than 40% in one sector is high concentration. If that company collapses, so does your portfolio.
concentracion.tip: Calculate your largest position. Is it more than 20% of your portfolio?

faq.q: How many free messages can I send?
faq.a: The Free plan includes 20 messages every 24 hours. With Premium you get unlimited messages and access to all advanced features.

faq.q: How does the 30-day free Premium work?
faq.a: All new users automatically get 30 days of free Premium, no credit card required. You can explore every feature with no restrictions during that period.

faq.q: How do I import my portfolio?
faq.a: In Portfolio you can paste a screenshot of your broker or add positions manually. The AI reads the image and extracts your positions, prices, and quantities automatically.

faq.q: How do I see the full analysis of a stock?
faq.a: Tap any stock from Portfolio, Watchlist, or Chat to open its full profile: historical chart, income statement, balance sheet, and cash flow with real-time data.

faq.q: How does the earnings calendar work?
faq.a: In the Portfolio section you'll find a calendar with the earnings report dates for your positions and Watchlist. It helps you anticipate important price moves.

It's a score (0-100) the AI calculates by analyzing your behavior: whether you panic during drops, whether you diversify well, whether you think long-term. It evolves as you use the app and shows up on your profile.

faq.q: Does the app sync between mobile and web?
faq.a: Yes. Your portfolio, watchlist, dark/light theme, and profile settings sync automatically between the app and the web version in real time.

faq.q: How do I cancel my Premium subscription?
faq.a: Go to Profile → Subscription → Cancel. Premium access stays active until the end of the period already paid for.

faq.q: How does the referral program work?
faq.a: You'll find your unique link in Profile. For every friend who signs up and uses the app, you earn weeks or months of free Premium.

q1.question: Your portfolio drops 35% in 3 months due to a market crisis. What do you do?

options.B: Wait for it to recover, but don't buy more
options.C: Check if the fundamentals still hold and stay put
options.D: Take the chance to buy more at low prices

q2.question: What do you need this invested money for, and in how long?

options.A: I might need it in less than 2 years
options.C: In 10+ years, for financial independence or retirement
options.D: No rush — it's to build long-term wealth

q3.question: Which of these concepts do you understand well enough to explain to someone else?

options.D: Fundamental analysis, derivatives hedging, market cycles

q4.question: You have $100,000 to invest. Which scenario do you prefer?

options.A: A guaranteed $5K gain, no chance of losing anything
options.B: A likely $15K gain, with risk of losing $5K
options.C: A possible $40K gain, with risk of losing $20K
options.D: A possible $120K gain, with risk of losing it all

q5.question: How much time would you dedicate to monitoring and managing your investments?

options.A: I prefer something automatic that needs no attention
options.B: A monthly or quarterly review seems enough
options.C: I like to check weekly and make adjustments when worth it
options.D: I'm willing to spend time on it daily — it's an active hobby

riskExtra.aggressive: You seek maximum growth. You tolerate high volatility in exchange for higher returns.

welcome.title: Welcome to Nuvos AI
welcome.subtitle: Your AI investment mentor
welcome.desc: Nuvos AI teaches you to think like a professional investor. We don't tell you what to buy — we teach you to analyze, understand, and make decisions on your own. All your data syncs in real time between the app and the web.
welcome.tip: 💡 Complete your initial profile so the AI can tailor its answers to your level: Beginner, Intermediate, or Advanced.

dashboard.title: Your Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: The market's pulse, at a glance
dashboard.desc: Home shows the S&P 500, Nasdaq, and Dow Jones indices in real time. Below, you'll see your portfolio's value with three key metrics: Today's performance, YTD (year to date), and cumulative Total.
dashboard.tip: 💡 The "Rising today" section shows the 4 positions in your portfolio with the biggest gain of the day.

chat.title: Chat with your AI Mentor
chat.subtitle: Nuvos's main tool
chat.desc: Ask about any company, ETF, concept, or strategy. The AI knows your risk profile, your actual portfolio, and your investor level, and detects when your decisions contradict them. Every conversation updates your behavioral maturity.
chat.tip: 💡 You can edit any of your messages by tapping the pencil icon that appears next to the text.

portfolio.title: Real-Time Portfolio
portfolio.subtitle: Your investments with live data
portfolio.desc: Add positions manually or with a screenshot of your broker — the AI extracts the data automatically. Prices update every 30 seconds. Switch between Basic and Advanced view to see Volume, Market Cap, P/E, and 52-week range.
portfolio.tip: 💡 The currency badge next to "My Portfolio" shows your portfolio's currency. Tap any position to see its full analysis.

calendar.title: Events Calendar
calendar.subtitle: Earnings, dividends, and ex-dividends
calendar.desc: The calendar in Watchlist automatically shows three types of events for all your positions and watchlist: Earnings (quarterly results), Ex-Dividend (deadline to receive the dividend), and Dividend payment.
calendar.tip: 💡 Your portfolio positions appear in bold green; watchlist ones in blue — so you can spot which ones affect you directly at a glance.

watchlist.title: Watchlist
watchlist.subtitle: Follow stocks without buying them
watchlist.desc: Add any stock to follow its price, news, and events in real time. In Advanced mode you get a full table with after-hours, market cap, P/E ratio, earnings date, and 52-week range.
watchlist.tip: 💡 Drag the cards to reorder your watchlist. The order syncs automatically between the app and the web.

learning.title: Learning & Tools
learning.subtitle: Everything you need to invest better
learning.desc: A library of financial concepts with AI tailored to your level: ETFs, fundamental analysis, P/E ratio, DCA, Value Investing, and investor psychology. The weekly Screener analyzes the market and selects personalized opportunities.
learning.tip: 💡 The Investors section shows you how the big funds invest — useful for spotting trends and validating your ideas.

profile.title: Your Profile & Investor Maturity
profile.subtitle: The AI that knows you as an investor
profile.desc: The AI analyzes your actual behavior — whether you panic, diversify well, or think long term — and assigns you an evolving Investor Maturity score (0-100). Your level (Beginner, Intermediate, or Advanced) tailors the whole experience.
profile.tip: 💡 Your behavioral risk bar in your profile updates automatically with every conversation. You can change your level in Profile at any time.

progressModal.title: Your Progress
progressModal.emptyGraph: Use chat and Play to see your evolution here
progressModal.signalsRecorded: {{count}} signals recorded · behavior in the app

paywallModal.heroFeature1: Understand what to do with your money, every day — no limits
paywallModal.heroFeature2: Know exactly how much you gained or lost, and why
paywallModal.heroFeature3: Never be surprised by news — we alert you before it moves your money
paywallModal.heroFeature4: Your portfolio built in 30 seconds, no spreadsheets
paywallModal.heroFeature5: 5 concrete opportunities every week, with reasoning and risk

f1.text: Ask whatever you want, whenever you want — no limit
f1.detail: An AI mentor available 24/7 that knows your real portfolio and answers with context on what you already have, not in the abstract.

f2.text: Be the first to know when one of your companies is about to report
f2.detail: Before quarterly results, the AI explains what to expect and if there's anything you should care about. No surprises.

f3.text: Find out if your money would survive another crisis like 2008 or COVID
f3.detail: Simulate your current portfolio against 30 real historical crises. See how much you would have lost and if you'd be comfortable with that.

f4.text: The AI honestly tells you what's wrong with your portfolio
f4.detail: Excessive concentration in one sector, lack of diversification, positions inconsistent with your profile. Concrete, not generic.

f5.text: Upload your statement and the AI builds everything for you
f5.detail: Upload a photo or PDF from your broker. The AI automatically detects your stocks, quantities, and purchase prices.

f6.text: Every Monday, 5 investment ideas tailored to your situation
f6.detail: No generic ideas. The AI considers what you already have, your profile, and what's happening in the market that week.

f7.text: We tell you why your money is moving — before you react
f7.detail: Notifications when something important moves one of your positions — with an explanation of why and whether you should do something.

f8.text: At the end of every month, know if you beat the market
f8.detail: Your real return vs the S&P 500. A personalized note on what worked and what didn't. No excuses, no technical jargon.

f9.text: Learn about the companies you already hold in your portfolio
f9.detail: Lessons designed for what you have. Before Apple reports, understand what to watch. Relevant, not generic.

f10.text: See how your portfolio compares to the market in real time
f10.detail: A simple panel: your performance today, this week, this year — against the S&P 500. Know at all times if you're doing well.

f11.text: Unlimited portfolio — no caps on stocks or watchlist
f11.detail: The free plan has a limit of 10. Premium removes that limit so your portfolio is fully yours.

f12.text: Nuvos knows you better over time — and remembers everything
f12.detail: With Premium you build your complete financial memory: behavior patterns, decision timeline, and portfolio evolution. The free plan only stores 10 basic beliefs.

metricHints.beta: Volatility relative to the market. Beta > 1 means it moves more than the S&P 500, for better or worse.
metricHints.debtEquity: How much debt the company has per dollar of shareholder equity. Lower is financially safer.
metricHints.divYield: Annual dividend as a % of the current price. It's what you get paid just for holding the stock.
metricHints.epsTtm: Earnings per share over the last 12 months. How much the company made per share issued.
metricHints.fcf: Free Cash Flow: real cash the company generates after its investments. More reliable than accounting earnings.
metricHints.marketCap: Total value of all outstanding shares in the market. Measures how big the company is.
metricHints.netMargin: Out of every $100 in sales, how much is left as final profit after taxes and all costs.
metricHints.grossMargin: Out of every $100 in sales, how much is left after subtracting just the direct cost of producing the product or service.
metricHints.operatingMargin: Out of every $100 in sales, how much is left as operating profit before interest and taxes.
metricHints.peFwd: P/E using next year's expected earnings. Reflects the market's expectations.
metricHints.peTtm: How many years of current earnings you're paying for each share. A high P/E means the market expects a lot of future growth.
metricHints.roe: How much the company earns for every dollar of capital shareholders put in. Higher means more efficient.
metricHints.roa: How much the company earns for every dollar of total assets it has, regardless of how they were financed (debt or equity).
metricHints.roic: How much return the company generates on all invested capital (debt + equity). Buffett's preferred metric for judging business quality.
metricHints.week52High: The highest price the stock reached in the last 12 months.
metricHints.week52Low: The lowest price the stock traded at in the last 12 months.
